tests.test_body:
declare void @foo(i64 noundef)

define void @pr59633(ptr noundef %ptr) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @pr59633(
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[PTR1:%.*]] = ptrtoint ptr [[PTR:%.*]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = trunc i64 [[PTR1]] to i4
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = zext i4 [[TMP0]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[WHILE_BODY:%.*]]
; CHECK:       while.body:
; CHECK-NEXT:    tail call void @foo(i64 noundef [[TMP1]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[WHILE_BODY]]
entry:
  br label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %ptr.addr.0 = phi ptr [ %ptr, %entry ], [ %add.ptr, %while.body ]
  %0 = ptrtoint ptr %ptr.addr.0 to i64
  %and = and i64 %0, 15
  tail call void @foo(i64 noundef %and)
  %add.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.addr.0, i64 16
  br label %while.body
}
tests.source_program:
declare void @foo(i64 noundef)

define void @pr59633(ptr noundef %ptr) {
entry:
  br label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %ptr.addr.0 = phi ptr [ %ptr, %entry ], [ %add.ptr, %while.body ]
  %0 = ptrtoint ptr %ptr.addr.0 to i64
  %and = and i64 %0, 15
  tail call void @foo(i64 noundef %and)
  %add.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.addr.0, i64 16
  br label %while.body
}
tests.current_optimized_program:
declare void @foo(i64 noundef)

define void @pr59633(ptr noundef %ptr) {
entry:
  br label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  %ptr.addr.0 = phi ptr [ %ptr, %entry ], [ %add.ptr, %while.body ]
  %0 = ptrtoint ptr %ptr.addr.0 to i64
  %and = and i64 %0, 15
  tail call void @foo(i64 noundef %and)
  %add.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr.addr.0, i64 16
  br label %while.body
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @foo(i64 noundef)

define void @pr59633(ptr noundef %ptr) {
entry:
  %ptr1 = ptrtoint ptr %ptr to i64
  %0 = trunc i64 %ptr1 to i4
  %1 = zext i4 %0 to i64
  br label %while.body

while.body:                                       ; preds = %while.body, %entry
  tail call void @foo(i64 noundef %1)
  br label %while.body
}

issue.title: Loop invariant expression using pointers not being optimized
issue.author: markoshorro

comments.author: vfdff
comments.body:
* For foo0: the %and is invariant
* For foo1: the  %0 is LoopComputable, so the %and is also LoopComputable ?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6094516/209328268-b4515a08-e0a2-4dab-8902-d3b8ef2b5174.png)

comments.author: markoshorro
comments.body:
@vfdff in your image what are you comparing? It is not very clear to me.

From my snippet above, you can see that:
* `foo0`: `%5 = and i64 %4, 15` is LoopComputable, because %4 -> %3 are also LoopComputable.
* `foo1`: the `and` is loop invariant

My point is: for `foo0` even though the cast `%4 = ptrtoint ptr %3 to i64` is required because of the handling of pointers in LLVM, couldn't it be also inferred that it is still loop invariant? What am I missing here?

comments.author: markoshorro
comments.body: Thank you very much for this @vfdff!
